explanations.2: .PHONY is a special Makefile keyword that tells Make these are not real files—they're just command names you can run.
explanations.4: The \e[1;37m and \e[m are escape codes that add bold white color to the terminal output; %-35s means 'left-align this text in a 35-character wide space'.
explanations.6: This printf line is malformed—it has four format specifiers (%s) but only provides three arguments, so the last part won't print correctly.
explanations.10: The @ symbol before a command hides that command from being printed to the terminal; without it, you'd see the command echoed before it runs.
